description_long: This object is a realistic crustacean, resembling a crayfish or small lobster, with a segmented body featuring a robust cephalothorax and a tapered abdomen. Its exoskeleton is colored in shades of brown and tan, with subtle gradations along the body and appendages. The crustacean has two prominent claws (chelae), one noticeably larger than the other, and several walking legs extending from the thoracic region. Long, thin antennae project from the head, and small, dark eyes are positioned on short stalks. The surface appears hard and slightly glossy, with a rough, textured finish typical of crustacean shells. The overall size is small, as indicated by the adjacent color calibration cube.
description: A small, brown-tan crustacean with segmented body, large claws, and long antennae, featuring a hard, textured shell.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the body, one large claw, and the extended antennae.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the segmentation of the body, both claws, and the symmetrical arrangement of the legs.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the other profile, with the smaller claw and the curvature of the tail.
description_view_3: Frontal view emphasizing the claws, antennae, and the forward-facing eyes on short stalks.
